title: Qualcomm Incorporated (QCOM) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Qualcomm Incorporated (QCOM). Economic Prospect Score: 65/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Qualcomm Incorporated is projected to grow FCF at 8% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 10.0% WACC balances Qualcomm Incorporated's earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisParagraphs: Qualcomm Incorporated (QCOM) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 8% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 10.0% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Qualcomm Incorporated's intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 8% FCF growth and 10.0% WACC, Opus calculates QCOM's intrinsic value at approximately $183.96 per share, suggesting the stock is fairly valued at $175.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive QCOM's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (8%) and discount rate (10.0%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: Qualcomm dominates mobile chip licensing and Snapdragon SoCs. The diversification into automotive, IoT, and PC chips (Snapdragon X) is promising. But Apple's modem development threatens the largest customer relationship, and the licensing model faces perpetual legal challenges.
prospectKeyRisks: Apple developing its own 5G modem, potentially eliminating Qualcomm's largest customer ($7-8B/yr)
Licensing model under perpetual legal pressure from OEMs and regulators globally
MediaTek competing effectively in mid-range smartphone chips, pressuring ASPs
prospectKeyCatalysts: Snapdragon X for Windows PCs opening a large new TAM as ARM-based laptops gain share
Automotive design wins ramping to $4B+ revenue as vehicles become software-defined platforms
AI inference on edge devices driving premium Snapdragon pricing and differentiation
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
